purpose: Implementation of PluginWindow. Owns a singleton Array<PluginWindow*> tracking every open editor; factories one window per (node, format type) lazily via getWindowFor — falling back from Normal → Generic when the plugin has no native editor. Restores last X/Y position from the node's properties and persists 'uiopen_<type>' state.
invariants: Normal-type lookups also match an existing Generic window for the same node — see comment at PluginWindow.cpp:137. This avoids creating two duplicate Generic windows when 'Edit' is invoked twice on a plugin that has no native editor.

Editor construction is wrapped in try-catch (std::exception + ...). Failures log and return nullptr; the graph node stays alive but no window opens. This guards against plugins whose UI throws on first paint.

JUCE 8.0.13+ uses createEditorAndMakeActive() (not createEditorIfNeeded() — the comment at line 155 calls this out). The method also registers the editor on the AudioProcessor so getActiveEditor() returns it.

getWindowFor returns nullptr in three cases: (1) node->getProcessor() returns nullptr — early-exit guard per backlog Fix #5; (2) editor creation throws; (3) all three editor factories produce nullptr.

closeButtonPressed sets the 'uiopen_<type>' property to false then deletes 'this' — the standard JUCE pattern. The destructor removes the pointer from getActiveWindows() so no dangling entries remain.
gotchas: createEditorAndMakeActive() can return nullptr for plugins with no native UI — when this happens for a Normal request, type is reassigned to Generic in the same call (line 160) and a GenericAudioProcessorEditor is constructed instead. The PluginWindow that gets stored therefore has type=Generic for such plugins, which is why the lookup at line 141 treats Normal/Generic as interchangeable.

ProgramAudioProcessorEditor's ProgramPropertyComponent::refresh() is a no-op — the program list is built once in the constructor. Plugin program-name changes after the window opens will not propagate; close and reopen the window to see them.

closeAllCurrentlyOpenWindows runs a 50ms modal dispatch loop only if JUCE_MODAL_LOOPS_PERMITTED is defined (it is, via CMake). The loop drains pending UI events so destroyed windows fully tear down before the caller continues.

dynamic_cast<AudioPluginInstance*> at line 184 is what gives the editor its plugin name in the title bar; DelayProcessor (the auto-inserted PDC node) is NOT an AudioPluginInstance — but it also has hasEditor()==false so getWindowFor will already return nullptr before reaching that line.
